trajectory_summary: The call books a sit on a genuinely warm, self-qualified lead — but the outcome is fragile because Bucky never built on the energy Dylan brought. The bill figure is unresolved and garbled, the appointment has a hard Friday-only constraint the closer must honor, and the button-up is incomplete (no 'is that fair' handshake, no one-hour block stated). The call shape is stagnant: Dylan's enthusiasm at open was never amplified, and the bill photo stall drained whatever momentum remained.
outcome_dispute: The call ends with a booked appointment (Friday the 1st at 3pm, phone call). However, the booking quality is low: the bill figure is unverified and garbled, the button-up is missing the verbal handshake and one-hour block, and the closer has no reliable bill anchor. The sit is booked but structurally thin.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Bill anchor depth — capturing the real figure when the customer self-corrects
primary_coaching_focus.quote: And full disclosure, because I've been traveling a lot for work lately, my, water consumption, my gas consumption, my electric is all way lower than it was, like, a year ago.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Dylan hands Bucky the actual bill anchor — his normal usage before travel depressed it — and Bucky responds with 'Yeah.' The closer will walk into this appointment without a reliable electric bill figure. The bill Bucky collected is a combined utilities figure (garbled by Deepgram on both utterances; closer must confirm directly), and Dylan has explicitly told us it is not representative. This is the single most consequential gap in the call: the closer's entire proposal is built on usage data that Bucky failed to capture correctly. In the 1-on-1, play this moment and ask Bucky: 'What did Dylan just tell you, and what question should you have asked next?' The target response is a follow-up that captures normal-usage electric bill — not a combined figure, not a travel-depressed figure. Do not use the garbled Deepgram figures as coaching anchors; flag them as unverified and have the closer confirm with Dylan directly.

key_moments.quote: I've got a pretty large south facing roof, and I figured that's why it wouldn't be a dumb choice to do in the future.
key_moments.stewart_read: Dylan volunteers a specific, qualifying detail — south-facing roof — before Bucky asks a single question. This is the emotional anchor the entire call should have been built on. A south-facing roof is a production advantage; Dylan already knows it. Bucky's response is to verify the address. The signal is walked past entirely.

key_moments.quote: $4.50 a month combined sewer, water, electricity, gas, normally.
key_moments.stewart_read: Deepgram renders this as '$4.50' — clearly garbled. Dylan is describing a combined utilities figure (sewer, water, electricity, gas), not a standalone electric bill. Bucky says 'Okay' and moves to the credit qualifier. No clarification of the electric-only figure, no emotional flip. The bill is captured as noise, not as an anchor.

key_moments.quote: No. I should be in the office then. Fridays generally work best for me.
key_moments.stewart_read: Dylan's Friday-only availability is a hard scheduling constraint. Bucky accommodates it correctly (books Friday the 1st at 3pm) but does not flag it as a constraint in any handoff context. The closer needs to know this appointment cannot be rescheduled to a weekday without risking a no-show.

key_moments.quote: And full disclosure, because I've been traveling a lot for work lately, my, water consumption, my gas consumption, my electric is all way lower than it was, like, a year ago.
key_moments.stewart_read: Dylan is doing Bucky's job for him — he is proactively flagging that the bill photo Bucky is about to receive is not representative of his normal usage. This is a trust gift and a bill-anchor self-correction. Bucky's response is 'Yeah.' and an immediate pivot to whether the photo sent. The gesture is not acknowledged, the normal-usage figure is not captured, and the emotional weight of 'I want to bring that down' (01:24) is never revisited.
